summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire, volcanic Loser, transcendent Terrapin/Space
blurb: The show delivers a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain, a volcanic Loser, and a soaring Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → Morning Dew suite. Jerry’s guitar stays solid despite a cold while Brent dazzles with a rare Muddy Waters cover. The second set burns with tight jams and seamless segues throughout.
review: Ventura County Fairgrounds on July 22, 1984 captured one of the Dead’s late‑summer high points. Kicking off with an electrifying "Dancin' In The Street," the first set built momentum through a punchy "Loser" (often called volcanic by fans) and a tight “Hell In A Bucket” jam before easing into the second half of the night. The second set is where the magic truly unfolds: a blistering "Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → Morning Dew" sequence that feels both cosmic and grounded, punctuated by a rare Brent‑led rendition of Muddy Waters’ "I Just Want To Make Love To You." The encore’s “In The Midnight Hour” flows straight into a heartfelt “Brokedown Palace,” with Garcia never leaving the stage between them – a detail many listeners cherish. Across all recordings the band sounds locked in; Jerry’s tone is warm and his vocal growl deep, Phil provides thunderous bass foundations, Brent’s keyboards add funk‑filled color, and Bill & Mickey drive a thunderous, yet nuanced, percussion section that lifts the improvisations to soaring heights.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All but one of the nine analyses are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the same highlights – volcanic Loser solo, scorching Scarlet>Fire segue, transcendent Terrapin/Space/Dawn suite and Brent's rare cover. The lone mixed review still notes high energy, so overall sentiment is overwhelmingly favorable, warranting a +1.6 boost.

best_recording.reason: Clean PCM soundboard source with virtually no hiss, captures the full band dynamics and segues; despite minor channel reversal quirks it remains the most detailed and reliable version of this show.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition
Volcanic Loser solo that drives the first set
Transcendent Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → Morning Dew suite
Rare Brent Mydland cover of "I Just Want To Make Love To You"

band_performance.Jerry: Solid playing despite a slight cold; his guitar tone is warm and his solo on "Loser" described as volcanic, while his vocals carry the deep growl that anchors the show.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is thunderous and driving, especially on "Dew" and "Loser," providing a powerful low‑end foundation throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Delivers a funky, standout rendition of Muddy Waters' "I Just Want To Make Love To You"; overall keyboard work is solid with tasteful fills, though some mix notes mention a forward hi‑hat presence.
band_performance.Mickey: Together with Bill, creates a cosmic "Drums → Space" segment praised for its power and precision, driving the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Provides tight groove and thunderous accents that lift the jams, especially during the Percussion → Improvisation → Morning Dew stretch.
